channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Onto the knockout round: 4 takeaways from the FIFA World Cup so far
item.description: The anxieties before the World Cup were many. But with the knockout round set to begin Sunday, it's fair to say: The North American World Cup has been a thrill.
item.content:encoded:
The anxieties before the World Cup were many. But with the knockout round set to begin Sunday, it's fair to say: The North American World Cup has been a thrill.

(Image credit: Patrick T. Fallon)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Trump nominates former Oklahoma state trooper to head ICE
item.description: President Trump nominated Lance Schroyer, a former Oklahoma state trooper, to direct Immigration and Customs Enforcement. The agency hasn't had a Senate-confirmed director since the Obama administration.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump nominated Lance Schroyer, a former Oklahoma state trooper, to direct Immigration and Customs Enforcement. The agency hasn't had a Senate-confirmed director since the Obama administration.

(Image credit: Erin Hooley)
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Critical fire weather complicates firefighting efforts in massive Utah wildfire
item.description: Firefighters working on the nation's largest current wildfire, burning in southern Utah, are being challenged by historic weather conditions. Extreme wildfire behavior is expected to continue through the weekend.
item.content:encoded:
Firefighters working on the nation's largest current wildfire, burning in southern Utah, are being challenged by historic weather conditions. Extreme wildfire behavior is expected to continue through the weekend.

(Image credit: Ty ONeil)
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Uzbekistan makes its World Cup debut, a first for Central Asia
item.description: The country is the first Central Asian nation to qualify for the World Cup, and Uzbek fans have reveled in showcasing their country and culture. The country's president calls the team a symbol of the "new Uzbekistan."
item.content:encoded:
The country is the first Central Asian nation to qualify for the World Cup, and Uzbek fans have reveled in showcasing their country and culture. The country's president calls the team a symbol of the "new Uzbekistan."

(Image credit: Ashley Landis)
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: Opinion: Ranch dressing is a winner at the World Cup games
item.description: NPR's Scott Simon reflects on the popularity of ranch dressing among international visitors to the U.S. during the World Cup games.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Simon reflects on the popularity of ranch dressing among international visitors to the U.S. during the World Cup games.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Trump administration partially lifts export ban on Anthropic's most advanced AI model
item.description: The U.S. government is asserting a new level of influence over AI, controlling which companies can access Anthropic's new models. OpenAI agreed to let the administration screen users of its new model.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. government is asserting a new level of influence over AI, controlling which companies can access Anthropic's new models. OpenAI agreed to let the administration screen users of its new model.
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: If a Lyme disease vaccine gets approved, how would it go over? We asked hunters
item.description: Drugmakers are working on a potential new shot to prevent the tick-borne illness. How might it fare in the era of vaccine skepticism?
item.content:encoded:
Drugmakers are working on a potential new shot to prevent the tick-borne illness. How might it fare in the era of vaccine skepticism?

(Image credit: Kyle Pyatt for KFF Health News)
item.dc:creator: Bram Sable-Smith

item.title: How coach Mauricio Pochettino made believers out of the U.S. World Cup team
item.description: Pochettino was the biggest name the U.S. men's soccer team had ever hired. His rebuild was bumpy at times — but now, with the U.S. headed to the World Cup knockout stage, the players are all in.
item.content:encoded:
Pochettino was the biggest name the U.S. men's soccer team had ever hired. His rebuild was bumpy at times — but now, with the U.S. headed to the World Cup knockout stage, the players are all in.

(Image credit: Russell Lewis)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: As Supreme Court expands Trump's immigration power, experts warn of steeper U.S. population decline
item.description: The U.S. population was already aging and tilting toward decline. After the Supreme Court confirmed Trump's power to deport hundreds of thousands of foreign migrants, population decline could accelerate.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. population was already aging and tilting toward decline. After the Supreme Court confirmed Trump's power to deport hundreds of thousands of foreign migrants, population decline could accelerate.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Inside a secretive Ukrainian team launching deep drone strikes at Russia
item.description: Ukraine's long-range drones are striking deep inside Russia, up to 1,200 miles away, hitting oil refineries and depots. NPR recently spent time with one of the Ukrainian strike teams launching drones at Russian targets.
item.content:encoded:
Ukraine's long-range drones are striking deep inside Russia, up to 1,200 miles away, hitting oil refineries and depots. NPR recently spent time with one of the Ukrainian strike teams launching drones at Russian targets.

(Image credit: Serhii Korovvayny for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis
